The afternoon will be more pleasant, but I still cannot rule out a shower through tonight. Monday is warmer and more humid with mostly afternoon and evening showers and thundershowers. Slow Improvement Sunday

Last Updated Sun May 24 at 11:26AM by Ray Russell

Fog with some drizzle and spotty light rain remains this morning, but the cool air will gradually scour today. The afternoon will be more pleasant, but I still cannot rule out a shower through tonight. Monday is warmer and more humid with mostly afternoon and evening showers and thundershowers. Showers and thundershowers may be more active Tuesday.

Forecast Discussion

The cool air pooled against the east side of the mountains continues to erode today. We have fog, patchy drizzle, and light showers this morning, but improving weather is the theme. Just don't expect sunshine and summer temperatures — "improving" is relative to yesterday's yuck. I can't rule out a shower tonight, especially on the east edge of the Appalachians.

Monday through Wednesday, a front will be to our north while high pressure in the Atlantic pumps warm, humid air into the Southern Appalachians. Showers and thundershowers develop each afternoon and evening — not limited to those hours, but showers and thundershowers are most likely during those hours. Tuesday looks like the most active of the three days.

The weather forecast becomes more uncertain after Wednesday. A cold front moves through from the north on Thursday and stalls to our south. Shower and thunderstorm activity probably diminishes Thursday — widely scattered — and backs off further Friday. A disturbance moving eastward along the front may increase shower activity Saturday.

Detailed forecast discussion and analysis is only available to RaysWeather supporters.

Latest Insights
Rainy, Cool Saturday

It's a rainy, cool Saturday around western North Carolina and Southwest Virginia. High temperatures are running as much as 20-degrees below normal in the heart of the cold air damming zone.

Sunday and Memorial Day will continue to be unsettled, but temperatures will moderate and there will be additional breaks in the clouds, as the rainfall pattern becomes more convective, or showery in nature.

While the rainfall is falling on our holiday weekend, it's entirely beneficial with respect to our long-standing drought conditions.
